2606.26963.title: Term-Centric Hierarchy Induction from Heterogeneous Corpora
2606.26963.paper_summary: Organizing knowledge from diverse text sources into interpretable hierarchies is crucial for tasks such as policy analysis, innovation monitoring, and exploratory domain mapping. Existing taxonomy induction methods typically rely on document-level representations that capture entire documents rather than the specific domain concepts relevant for knowledge organization, limiting their ability to generalize across heterogeneous sources. We propose a term-centric framework for inducing hierarchical taxonomies from heterogeneous corpora that scales to massive document collections. Our approach maps documents from diverse sources into a shared representation space using automatic term extraction, enabling robust cross-source alignment. Based on these representations, we construct interpretable hierarchies that integrate domain priors with datadriven clustering. Experiments on a novel English and German multi-source benchmark of over one million documents demonstrate that our method improves cross-source coherence and hierarchy quality over text- and summarybased baselines. A case study on German regional innovation analysis further demonstrates its practical utility for technology landscape mapping.
2606.26963.paper_summary_zh: 將來自不同文本來源的知識組織成可解釋的層次結構對於政策分析、創新監測和探索性領域映射等任務至關重要。現有的分類法誘導方法通常依賴於文檔級別的表示，這些表示捕捉整個文檔，而不是與知識組織相關的特定領域概念，這限制了它們在異質來源之間的泛化能力。我們提出了一種以術語為中心的框架，旨在從異質語料庫中誘導層次分類法，並能擴展到大規模文檔集合。我們的方法通過自動術語提取將來自不同來源的文檔映射到共享表示空間，從而實現穩健的跨來源對齊。基於這些表示，我們構建了可解釋的層次結構，將領域先驗與數據驅動的聚類相結合。在一個超過一百萬篇文檔的新英語和德語多來源基準上的實驗表明，我們的方法在文本和摘要基準之上提高了跨來源的一致性和層次質量。對德國區域創新分析的案例研究進一步展示了其在技術景觀映射中的實際效用。
2606.26963.author: Elena Senger et.al.
2606.26963.authors: Elena Senger, Yuri Campbell, Jan-Peter Bergmann, Rob van der Goot, Barbara Plank
